title: Katha Upanishad I: Naciketas Confronts Death
description: From the Katha Upanishad I — one of the foundational texts of Indian philosophy.
introduction: From the Katha Upanishad I — one of the foundational texts of Indian philosophy.

translation.text: When man dies, there is this doubt — some say he is, others say he is not. Teach me, O Death. Knowing this is my third boon. Death said: Even the gods of old doubted this. Choose another boon. Naciketas said: If even the gods doubted, who better to teach me than Death himself?
